title: default clauses should be first or last
description: The switch statement is a conditional statement that executes a sequence of instructions based on patterns matching the provided value. switch (temperatureInCelsius) Console.WriteLine("Hypothermia"); case >= 36.5 and <= 37.5: Console.WriteLine("Normal"); case > 37.5 and <= 40.0: Console.WriteLine("Fever or hyperthermia"); Console.WriteLine("Hyperpyrexia"); The switch statement can optionally contain a default clause, executed when none of the case clauses are executed (or in presence of a goto default;). Console.WriteLine("Excellent"); Console.WriteLine("Very Good"); Console.WriteLine("Good"); Console.WriteLine("Pass"); Console.WriteLine("Fail"); Console.WriteLine("Invalid grade letter!"); The default clause can be defined for various reasons: to handle unexpected values, as shown in the example above or to show that all the cases were properly considered, making the function explicitely total (as opposed to partial) While C# allows the default clause to appear in any place within a switch statement, and while its position doesn’t alter its behavior, it is recommended to put the default clause either at the beginning or at the end of the switch statement.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detect switch statements with default clause not first or last
logic.triggering_conditions: default: clause appears in middle of switch (has case before AND after)
logic.exclusion_conditions: Exclude default at beginning or end
logic.reasoning: default clause position doesn't affect behavior but placing it in the middle reduces readability. Best practice is first or last position. Detects default: with case clauses both before and after it.

should_trigger: switch (x) { case 1: break; default: break; case 2: break; }
switch (status) { case A: break; case B: break; default: break; case C: break; }
should_not_trigger: switch (x) { case 1: break; case 2: break; default: break; }
switch (x) { default: break; case 1: break; case 2: break; }

checks.message: Move default clause to the beginning or end of the switch statement
